2604.22905.title: CT-Guided Spatially-varying Regularization for Voxel-Wise Deformable Whole-Body PET Registration
2604.22905.paper_summary: Whole-body Positron Emission Tomography (PET) registration is essential for multi-parametric tumor characterization and assessment of metastatic disease progression. In deep learning-based deformable registration, the dense displacement field (DDF) regularizer is crucial for stabilizing optimization and preventing unrealistic deformations in large 3D volumes. A key challenge in whole-body deformable registration is anatomical heterogeneity, rigid structures (e.g., bones) should undergo stronger regularization, whereas soft tissues require more flexible deformation and weaker constraints. In this work, we propose a simple yet effective CT-guided spatially-varying regularization strategy for whole-body cross-tracer deformable PET registration. The key idea is to use the paired CT volume from the PET/CT acquisition to construct a voxel-wise regularization map for the DDF, replacing the conventional single global regularization weight. This yields anatomy-adaptive regularization strength across rigid and soft tissues. The proposed method is evaluated on a real clinical cross-tracer PET/CT dataset of 296 patients involving 18F-PSMA and 18F-FDG, showing that the proposed method achieves statistically significant improvements over weakly-supervised registration baseline in both whole-body registration performance and organ-wise alignment.
2604.22905.paper_summary_zh: 全身正電子發射斷層掃描（PET）配準對於多參數腫瘤特徵化和轉移性疾病進展評估至關重要。 在基於深度學習的可變形配準中，密集位移場（DDF）正則化器對於穩定優化和防止大型3D體積中的不現實變形至關重要。 全身可變形配準的一個主要挑戰是解剖異質性，剛性結構（例如，骨骼）應該經歷更強的正則化，而軟組織則需要更靈活的變形和較弱的約束。 在這項工作中，我們提出了一種簡單而有效的CT引導空間變化正則化策略，用於全身交叉示蹤劑的可變形PET配準。 主要思想是使用來自PET/CT獲取的配對CT體積來構建DDF的體素級正則化圖，取代傳統的單一全局正則化權重。 這產生了對剛性和軟組織的解剖自適應正則化強度。 提出的方法在一個包含296名患者的真實臨床交叉示蹤劑PET/CT數據集上進行評估，涉及18F-PSMA和18F-FDG，顯示該方法在全身配準性能和器官對齊方面相較於弱監督配準基線實現了統計上顯著的改進。
2604.22905.author: Xiangcen Wu et.al.
2604.22905.authors: Xiangcen Wu, Ruohua Chen, Sichun Li, Qianye Yang, Sheng Liu, Jianjun Liu, Zhaoheng Xie
